articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Zimbabwe’s Central Bank Says Its Currency Deeply Undervalued
more.description: Despite efforts at stabilization and reduced inflation, over 90% of transactions in Zimbabwe are still conducted in US dollars. Inflation has dropped to single digits for the first time in nearly 30 years, though annual inflation slightly increased to 4.4% in March. Zimbabwe’s central bank governor says the country’s currency is significantly undervalued, arguing that […]The post Zimbabwe’s Central Bank Says Its Currency Deeply Undervalued appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Health minister Mombeshora pleads with striking nurses to return to work
more.description: HARARE — Health minister Douglas Mombeshora on Monday appealed to striking nurses to suspend their job action on the first day of a three-day walkout over pay. Nurses walked out at hospitals across the country, accusing the government of paying lip service to salary demands. They earn an average of US$290 and ZiG5,000 per month, […]The post Health minister Mombeshora pleads with striking nurses to return to work appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Zanu-PF Youth League Trashes Chivayo's U.S.$3.6 Million Donation - Says Parliament Should Not Be Bought
more.description: [New Zimbabwe] In a surprise turn of events, Zanu PF's Youth League has released a scathing statement trashing shadowy businessman Wicknell Chivayo's US$3.6 million donation to Parliament.

more.title: Mutapa Targets US$475m Resource-Backed Funding as 2025 Accounts Raise Fresh Concerns
more.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe’s sovereign wealth vehicle, the Mutapa Investment Fund, is seeking to raise up to US$475 million using its mining assets, even as its latest financial results reveal underlying weaknesses in cash generation, governance, and balance sheet quality. According to NewWire, Mutapa plans to secure US$75 million through a domestic syndicated facility with local […]The post Mutapa Targets US$475m Resource-Backed Funding as 2025 Accounts Raise Fresh Concerns appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Drone Solutions Zimbabwe Launches Rapid Medical Drone Delivery Network to Reach Remote Communities
more.description: HARARE — Zimbabwean technology firm Drone Solutions Zimbabwe has launched a drone-powered logistics network aimed at improving the delivery of medical supplies to hard-to-reach communities across the country. The initiative, developed in partnership with Drone University, a Civil Aviation Authority of Zimbabwe (CAAZ)-licensed operator training institution, is designed to overcome longstanding infrastructure challenges, including seasonal […]The post Drone Solutions Zimbabwe Launches Rapid Medical Drone Delivery Network to Reach Remote Communities appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: IMF warns South Africa of recession
more.description: JOHANNESBURG – The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has warned that the global economy faces recession if the conflict in the Middle East continues—with energy-importing countries like South Africa at even greater risk. In a report published last week, IMF economists warned that a longer or broader conflict could significantly weaken growth and destabilise financial markets. […]The post IMF warns South Africa of recession appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Arteta says Arsenal ‘more convinced’ about winning title despite loss to Man City
more.description: MANCHESTER, England — Arsenal’s players are “more convinced” than ever that they can win the Premier League despite losing to title rival Manchester City on Sunday, manager Mikel Arteta said. more.title: How Bayern Munich won its 13th Bundesliga title in 14 years
more.description: BERLIN — Bayern Munich wrapped up another Bundesliga title on Sunday, the club’s 13th in 14 years, after a record-breaking season that could still get better. more.title: Oil prices jump and stocks are mixed as the US-Iran standoff keeps the Strait of Hormuz in limbo
more.description: Oil prices climbed more than 5% while world shares were mixed Monday as a standoff between Iran and the U.S. prevented tankers from using the Strait of Hormuz. more.title: Benjani unpaid for three months as Highlanders sponsorship payment system breaks down
more.description: Highlanders FC coach Benjani Mwaruwari has reportedly gone three months without pay, highlighting growing concerns over the club’s sponsorship payment system. more.title: Army colonels block eviction at Mazowe farm in tense standoff with Sheriff
more.description: Two army colonels allegedly blocked the Sheriff of the High Court from carrying out an eviction at a disputed farm in Mazowe, triggering a tense standoff that lasted several hours. more.title: Caledonia reports Q1 gold output decline at Blanket Mine but maintains 2026 production forecast
more.description: Caledonia Mining Corporation Plc has reported a decline in gold production at its Blanket Mine in Zimbabwe for the first quarter of 2026, citing operational constraints and planned mining sequencing, while maintaining its full-year output forecast.
